flashcards.front: What is an Input Device in Computer Graphics?
flashcards.back: An input device is hardware that allows users to enter data and commands into a computer system for processing. In computer graphics, input devices enable users to create, manipulate, and interact with visual content digitally.

flashcards.front: What is an Output Device in Computer Graphics?
flashcards.back: An output device displays or presents processed data from the computer to the user in visual, audio, or physical form. In computer graphics, output devices render images, animations, and visual designs for viewing and printing.

flashcards.front: Define DPI (Dots Per Inch) and its significance in output devices
flashcards.back: DPI measures the resolution of printed or displayed images, indicating how many dots a printer or display can produce per inch. Higher DPI results in sharper, more detailed output, with 300 DPI being standard for quality print documents and 72 DPI typical for screen display.

flashcards.front: What is a Graphics Tablet (Digitizer) and its applications?
flashcards.back: A graphics tablet is an input device with a flat surface that detects stylus or pen movement for precise digital drawing and design work. It is essential for computer graphics professionals, digital artists, and CAD applications due to its pressure sensitivity and accuracy.

flashcards.front: Explain Refresh Rate in monitors and its importance
flashcards.back: Refresh rate, measured in Hz, indicates how many times per second a monitor redraws the screen image. Higher refresh rates (60Hz, 120Hz, 144Hz) provide smoother motion and reduce eye strain, crucial for graphics work and gaming applications.

flashcards.front: What is a 3D Scanner and how is it used in graphics?
flashcards.back: A 3D scanner captures the shape and appearance of real objects to create digital 3D models for computer graphics. It is widely used in reverse engineering, animation, game development, and virtual reality to accurately replicate physical objects digitally.

flashcards.front: Compare Inkjet and Laser printers for graphics output
flashcards.back: Inkjet printers spray liquid ink onto paper, ideal for color photographs and photo-quality prints with lower initial cost. Laser printers use toner and heat, providing faster, sharper text and graphics for high-volume printing with lower per-page costs.

flashcards.front: What are VR Headsets and their role in computer graphics?
flashcards.back: VR headsets are output devices that display stereoscopic 3D images to create immersive virtual reality experiences. In computer graphics, they are used for virtual walkthroughs, simulations, gaming, and interactive 3D modeling environments.
